content: WebRTC topology for sensor streaming: P2P mesh gives lowest latency (0-2ms on LAN, 50-150ms internet) at near-zero server cost but breaks above 4-5 peers due to N-1 upload explosion. SFU routes each sender's stream once, enabling 5-100+ peers with manageable bandwidth. MCU is inappropriate for data channels. For IMU sensor streaming (small packets, many sends, fire-and-forget), P2P direct connection is optimal for the primary phone→desktop pair. SFU becomes relevant only when one phone must stream to multiple desktops simultaneously. The architecture should default to P2P with TURN fallback, not SFU, because data channel payloads are tiny and the overhead of an SFU relay adds a server hop latency (10-50ms) without any mixing benefit.
